annotations.label: Optimum attained on boundary when bounded

anchor.snippet: if a feasible solution exists and if the constraint set is bounded, then the optimum value is always attained on the boundary

annotations.label: Convex combination of solutions is a solution

anchor.snippet: if there are two distinct solutions, then every convex combination of the solutions is a solution
The vertices of the polytope are also called basic feasible solutions

The simplex algorithm , developed by George Dantzig in 1947, solves LP problems by constructing a feasible solution at a vertex
The simplex algorithm has been proved to solve "random" problems efficiently, i.e. in a cubic number of steps
Klee and Minty constructed a family of linear programming problems for which the simplex method takes a number of steps exponential in the problem size
the criss-cross algorithm is a basis-exchange algorithm that pivots between bases
Both algorithms visit all 2 D corners of a (perturbed) cube in dimension D
interior-point methods move through the interior of the feasible region

If all of the unknown variables are required to be integers, then the problem is called an integer programming
integer programming problems are in many practical situations (those with bounded variables) NP-hard
0–1 integer programming or binary integer programming (BIP) is the special case of integer programming where variables are required to be 0 or 1
If only some of the unknown variables are required to be integers, then the problem is called a mixed integer (linear) programming
some important subclasses of IP and MIP problems that are efficiently solvable, most notably problems where the constraint matrix is totally unimodular
A linear program in real variables is said to be integral if it has at least one optimal solution which is integral
is said to be integral if for all bounded feasible objective functions c
